title: He Built $100B at Google, Then Failed. Now He Runs an $80B+ Company | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
summary: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, shares lessons from his tenure scaling Google's advertising business, his failed search engine startup Neeva, and his approach to leading Snowflake into the AI era. He emphasizes that founders and leaders must remain close to product development, hire adaptable teams, and recognize that both failures and successes do not define their identity.
writeup: Snowflake CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy discusses lessons drawn from three phases of his career: scaling Google's advertising business, founding the search startup Neeva that did not succeed, and now leading Snowflake through the AI era. The conversation focuses on leadership decisions that carry across those contexts, including staying close to product development and hiring teams that can adapt. Key takeaways: founders and leaders should not delegate away product involvement, adaptability matters more than a fixed team, and neither failures nor successes should be treated as a definition of personal identity.
